integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Connect your tools and services to enable AI-powered incident investigations

card.reauthorizeTooltip: Refresh OAuth permissions — use this if scopes or access have changed

empty.title: No integrations found
empty.description: Try adjusting your search or filter

modal.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
modal.oauthComingSoon: OAuth integration coming soon. This integration will use secure OAuth authorization — no manual tokens needed.
modal.oauthDescription: You will be redirected to {name} to authorize access. No manual tokens needed.
modal.awsSetupInfo: Use these values when creating the IAM role trust policy:

fields.privateKeyPlaceholder: Paste your PEM private key here

validation.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address

steps.1: Open the AWS IAM Console and go to Roles.
steps.2: Click "Create role" and select "Another AWS account".
steps.3: Enter the CauseFlow AWS Account ID and enable "Require external ID".
steps.4: Paste the External ID shown below into the trust policy.
steps.5: Attach the AWS managed policy ReadOnlyAccess (provides read-only access across all AWS services). If you don't see it in the policy list, change the filter to 'AWS managed - job function'.
steps.6: Name the role (e.g. CauseFlowReadOnly) and copy the Role ARN.

steps.1: Go to api.slack.com/apps and click "Create New App".
steps.2: Choose "From scratch" and name it (e.g. CauseFlow).
steps.3: Under OAuth & Permissions, add Bot Token Scopes: channels:read, users:read, groups:read.
steps.4: Click "Install to Workspace" and authorize the app.
steps.5: Copy the Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-).

steps.2: Click "Create API token" and give it a label (e.g. CauseFlow).
steps.4: Enter your Atlassian email and the domain URL (e.g. https://yourcompany.atlassian.net).

steps.1: Go to trello.com/power-ups/admin and log in.
steps.2: Click "New" to create a new Power-Up/Integration.
steps.3: Go to the API Key section and generate your API Key.
steps.4: Click the "Token" link next to your API key to generate a read-only token.
steps.5: Copy both the API Key and Token.

steps.1: Go to github.com/settings/apps and click "New GitHub App".
steps.2: Name it (e.g. CauseFlow) and set the Homepage URL.
steps.3: Under Permissions, set Repository permissions to Read-only for: Contents, Issues, Pull requests, Metadata.
steps.4: Generate a private key and download the PEM file.
steps.5: Install the app on your organization or repositories.
steps.6: Note the App ID (from app settings) and Installation ID (from the URL after installing).

steps.1: Go to HubSpot Settings > Integrations > Private Apps.
steps.2: Click "Create a private app" and name it (e.g. CauseFlow).
steps.3: Under Scopes, select read-only access for: crm.objects.contacts.read, crm.objects.deals.read, tickets.
steps.4: Click "Create app" and copy the Access Token (starts with pat-).

steps.1: Go to sentry.io and navigate to Settings > Auth Tokens.
steps.5: Enter your organization slug (visible in the Sentry URL).

slack.saveError: Failed to save Slack configuration. Please try again.
slack.channelError: Channel must start with # and contain only lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores

sentrySetup.title: Configure Sentry Internal Integration
sentrySetup.subtitle: Sentry's Internal Integration emits a Client Secret used to sign every webhook with HMAC-SHA256. Follow the steps below carefully.
sentrySetup.webhookUrlHelp: Paste this exact URL into Sentry's Webhook URL field.
sentrySetup.clientSecretPlaceholder: Paste the Client Secret displayed by Sentry
sentrySetup.clientSecretHelp: We forward this once to our backend and never store it in your browser. Required to verify webhooks.
sentrySetup.successToast: Sentry Client Secret saved. Awaiting first event.
sentrySetup.errorToast: Failed to save Sentry Client Secret: {message}

steps.1: In Sentry, go to Settings → Integrations.
steps.4: In Webhook URL, paste the value displayed above.
steps.5: Permissions: set Issue & Event = Read and Webhooks = Read.
steps.6: Webhooks (issue): subscribe to created, resolved, assigned, archived, unresolved.
steps.7: Save → copy the displayed Client Secret and paste it below.

webhookSetup.title: Configure {name} webhook
webhookSetup.instructions: Paste the URL below into your {name} webhook settings to start receiving alerts.

steps.1: Open Sentry and navigate to Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
steps.3: Paste the URL above into the 'Callback URLs' field.
steps.4: Save the webhook. Sentry will POST every new issue event to CauseFlow.

steps.1: Open PagerDuty and navigate to Integrations → Generic Webhooks (v3).
steps.3: Paste the URL above into the 'Webhook URL' field.
steps.4: Set the scope to the service(s) you want CauseFlow to investigate.
steps.5: Save. CauseFlow will receive every incident.triggered event.

steps.1: Open Datadog and navigate to Integrations → Webhooks.
steps.2: Click '+ New' to add a webhook. Name it 'causeflow'.
steps.3: Paste the URL above into the 'URL' field.
steps.5: In each Monitor that should trigger an investigation, add @webhook-causeflow to the notification message.

disconnect.title: Disconnect {name}
disconnect.warning: This will remove the connection to {name}. Ongoing investigations using this integration may be affected.
